title: Urostigma tomentosum
taxonomy: Urostigma tomentosum is classified under the family of Moraceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the mulberry or fig family. The genus Urostigma is a group of figs native to tropical regions. Urostigma tomentosum is further divided into the species of the same name. However, it is worth noting that Urostigma is an outdated or synonym name for the genus Ficus. The correct classification is therefore Ficus species within the family Moraceae.
uses: Urostigma tomentosum, also known as the Polynesian tree fern, has been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves are also used to create a tea that is believed to have antibacterial and antifungal properties. Research has shown that Urostigma tomentosum contains compounds with potential antioxidant and antiviral activities. More studies are needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of this plant.
